# Daily Operating Rhythm

## Recommended time blocks (adjust to your timezone)

| Time | Action |
|------|--------|
| **Morning (15–20 min)** | Scan: Massie posts overnight; KY headlines; one national story if relevant to KY-4 or the principal’s known issues. |
| **Midday (10 min)** | Queue: move one idea from “idea” to “draft” OR reply to one high-signal thread. |
| **Evening (15–20 min)** | Publish: post approved content; log in tracker; note engagement worth following up tomorrow. |

**Minimum viable day:** 1 substantive interaction (reply, quote, or original) + monitoring.

## Weekly rhythm

| Day | Focus |
|-----|--------|
| **Monday** | Set theme for the week (optional): one pillar from mission (war powers / corruption / cartel economy + border-liberty). |
| **Wednesday** | Mid-week thread or long-form post if you have bandwidth. |
| **Friday** | Week recap or “best of” quote; update KPI sheet. |

## Coordination with drafters

If someone else drafts copy:

1. They deliver **text + links** in your agreed channel (email, doc, chat).
2. You **edit for voice** and **platform fit** (length, thread breaks).
3. You **publish** under your account responsibility.

## Incident handling

- **Wrong screenshot / bad link:** Delete if misleading; repost corrected with brief note if needed.
- **Viral negative attention:** Pause new posts; escalate to operator; do not argue in circles for hours.

## “Good morning” habit (if your team uses it)

Some teams start the day with a **brief + draft candidates** from research. Your job: **curate** — not everything becomes a post. Pick 1–2 items that fit the mission and are sourceable.
